text: fix UI bug, where phase scrolls into view up and then down (or down and up), if it's too large to fit the screen. (on column navigation)

reflections.context: Open aim reporting up-then-down scroll jitter for oversized phases on column navigation. Created 2026-06-14.
reflections.outcome: Found it was already resolved by commit 5f74ed1 (2026-06-22). Column.vue funnels phase-level and aim-level scrolls through one requestAnimationFrame coalescer with priority (aim > phase) and a 300ms hold that drops the deferred realign. Marked done; did not change code.
reflections.effectiveness: Verified via git history (dedicated fix commit dated after the aim) and passing useScrollIntoView.spec.ts. Did not write code.
reflections.lesson: Check git log for the affected files before implementing — an aim opened before a same-named fix commit is usually already done, just left open. Status hygiene lags the actual work.

status.comment: Already fixed by commit 5f74ed1 (2026-06-22), 8 days after this aim was opened. Column.vue now routes all programmatic scrolls through a single rAF coalescer (requestScroll) with aim>phase priority + a 300ms AIM_SCROLL_HOLD that suppresses the late realign, so the two competing smooth animations that caused the up-then-down jitter can no longer both fire. useScrollIntoView.spec.ts passes (3/3). Visual jitter is inherently interactive — reopen if it recurs.
